chief_complaint: An 11-year-old Iranian girl presented with abdominal and flank pain, fever, and fatigue.
history: The patient had poorly controlled type 1 diabetes and was initially admitted with a diagnosis of diabetic ketoacidosis. Despite management for diabetic ketoacidosis, the patient continued to exhibit signs of fever and illness. Further evaluation ruled out diabetic ketoacidosis, and active urine analysis indicated a kidney pathology.
exam: Vital signs: fever, abdominal and flank pain. Physical examination findings: fatigue.
labs: Computed tomography scan: emphysematous pyelonephritis (stage 4), gas within the pyelocaliceal system. Urine analysis: kidney pathology. Bloods: results not specified.
